DY.headline: Why Is Dycom Stock Skyrocketing Wednesday?
DY.description: Dycom Industries (DY) stock surged 27.95% to a new 52-week high after reporting strong Q1 earnings that beat expectations. Contract revenue rose 56.1% YoY to $1.97B, adjusted earnings jumped 84.9% to $4.42 per share, and the company raised its fiscal 2027 revenue guidance to $7.38-$7.65B. The growth was driven by robust demand for fiber infrastructure and data center construction. Dycom also announced a $275M acquisition of National Technology Integrators to expand its digital infrastructure cap

DY.headline: Why Dycom Industries Stock Exploded Today
DY.description: Dycom Industries stock surged 27.12% after beating analyst forecasts with $4.42 EPS on $2 billion in sales, significantly exceeding Wall Street's expectations of $2.72 EPS on $1.7 billion. The company raised full-year guidance to $7.5 billion in revenue, driven by record demand for fiber infrastructure and data center builds in the AI era. With a 46.5% year-over-year increase in backlog to $11.9 billion, the company is positioned for continued strong growth.

DY.headline: Dycom Industries, Inc. Reports Record First Quarter Results and Raises Full Year Fiscal 2027 Outlook
DY.description: Dycom Industries reported record first quarter fiscal 2027 results with contract revenues of $1.965 billion (56.1% growth, 24.7% organic) and net income of $91.3 million. The company raised its full year fiscal 2027 outlook and announced the acquisition of National Technology Integrators for $275 million to expand capabilities in the high-growth data center industry. Strong demand for fiber infrastructure and data center builds drove performance across both segments.

LUNR.headline: Space Stocks Are Flying Ahead Of NASA Moon Base Event, SpaceX IPO
LUNR.description: Space stocks rallied on Tuesday as investors positioned ahead of NASA's Moon Base press conference and digested SpaceX's recent S-1 IPO filing. The momentum is being driven by expectations of government-backed space spending, lunar infrastructure contracts, and renewed investor appetite for space sector names. Key beneficiaries include lunar lander companies, satellite imaging firms, and space infrastructure providers.

LUNR.headline: Why Intuitive Machines Stock Keeps Going Up
LUNR.description: Intuitive Machines stock surged 17.2% after Cantor Fitzgerald raised its price target by 65% to $43 per share. The rally is driven by expectations of a potential $4.6 billion NASA Lunar Terrain Vehicle contract award this week, combined with the company's promise to reach $1 billion in revenue and profitability this year. Additional catalysts include an upcoming moon landing and potential Space Force contracts worth $1.8-6.2 billion over 10 years.

LUNR.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Up Amid US 'Defensive Strikes' On Iran— Elbit Systems, Intuitive Machines, Zscaler In Focus (UPDATED)
LUNR.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Tuesday following Memorial Day, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 gaining 0.52% and 0.73% respectively. Despite U.S. defensive strikes on Iranian missile sites, markets remained resilient as oil prices fell and Iran negotiations progressed. Key movers included Elbit Systems jumping 8.26% on a $1.4B military contract, Intuitive Machines soaring 10.27% on a NASA lunar contract, and SCYNEXIS plunging 16.38% after announcing a reverse stock split.

RDW.headline: The SpaceX IPO Frenzy Is Creating 2 Very Different Bets
RDW.description: The anticipated SpaceX IPO is driving capital into the space sector, creating divergent investment opportunities. AST SpaceMobile is experiencing a retail-driven momentum rally fueled by short squeezes and partnerships with major telecom companies, but faces valuation risks (660x sales) and dilution from a $1 billion convertible note issuance. In contrast, Redwire Corp shows signs of sophisticated institutional accumulation through unusual options activity, supported by a strong balance sheet, d

RDW.headline: US Stocks Show AI Breadth Is Replacing Defensive Leadership
RDW.description: US equities rallied post-Memorial Day with strong AI and small-cap leadership, as de-escalation hopes in the US-Iran conflict drove oil prices lower. Micron Technology surged 18% to cross $1 trillion market cap on a UBS price target hike, while quantum computing and space stocks rallied on government funding and SpaceX IPO anticipation. The Nasdaq rose 1.11% and Russell 2000 jumped 1.74%, though the Dow declined 0.19% as defensive sectors unwound.

RDW.headline: Space Stocks Are Flying Ahead Of NASA Moon Base Event, SpaceX IPO
RDW.description: Space stocks rallied on Tuesday as investors positioned ahead of NASA's Moon Base press conference and digested SpaceX's recent S-1 IPO filing. The momentum is being driven by expectations of government-backed space spending, lunar infrastructure contracts, and renewed investor appetite for space sector names. Key beneficiaries include lunar lander companies, satellite imaging firms, and space infrastructure providers.

UMC.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow Futures Rise Following Tech Rally—Zscaler, United Microelectronics, Braiin In Focus (UPDATED)
UMC.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following a mixed Tuesday session where the Nasdaq advanced led by tech stocks while the Dow ended lower. Key movers include United Microelectronics jumping 11.24% after acquiring manufacturing equipment, Braiin tumbling 13.45% after a 74% surge, Box declining 2.42% despite beating earnings expectations, and Zscaler falling after missing revenue guidance. The 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.46%, with markets pricing a 99.9% likelihood of unchanged Fed rate

VICR.headline: Here's Why Aehr Test Systems Popped Higher Today
VICR.description: Aehr Test Systems stock surged 16.2% following Vicor's unexpected raise in second-quarter revenue guidance. Vicor's strength in AI infrastructure power systems signals accelerating demand for data center solutions, which benefits Aehr's wafer-level burn-in test equipment business. Aehr recently received a $41 million order from a hyperscaler customer, bringing total orders to over $92 million for the second half of fiscal 2026, exceeding previous guidance.

MU.headline: The Memory Shortage Powering Micron's $1 Trillion Run Is Lifting These Stocks, Too
MU.description: Micron Technology reached a $1 trillion market capitalization as AI demand creates a severe shortage of memory chips. The same supply crunch is benefiting Sandisk and Western Digital, which were spun off from each other in February 2025. Sandisk's NAND flash storage revenue tripled with gross margins reaching 78.4%, while Western Digital's high-capacity drives are sold out through 2026 with customer agreements extending to 2028-2029. However, analysts caution that memory and storage remain cycli

MU.headline: Could Micron Technology Stock Hit More Than $1,600?
MU.description: Micron Technology reached a $1 trillion market cap after UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri set a $1,625 price target, citing the stock's modest 9x P/E ratio. However, the consensus price target is only $570, suggesting potential 38% downside risk. While the stock has surged 880% over the past year on AI-driven demand, analysts caution that Micron's historically volatile memory business may not sustain current growth levels, making it risky at current valuations.

MU.headline: Dow Jones Hit Records Above 50,600 As Oil Sinks Below $90: Stock Market Today
MU.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average reached a record high above 50,600 while the Nasdaq 100 declined 0.7% on Wednesday. Oil prices fell sharply below $90 per barrel following reports of potential Iran negotiations, benefiting travel and leisure stocks. The S&P 500 edged slightly lower as semiconductor stocks retreated, while consumer discretionary and hospitality sectors led gains.

MU.headline: Something Rare Is Powering The Russell 2000's Record Run, And AI Is The Reason
MU.description: The Russell 2000 index is reaching record highs driven by AI-related stocks rather than regional banks, which historically led small-cap rallies. Technology and industrial stocks account for over 70% of the index's year-to-date gains, with semiconductor, optical equipment, and data-center infrastructure companies surging. The AI trade has moved downstream to suppliers and builders supporting data-center buildout.

MU.headline: Nio Stock Rallies Wednesday, Shaking Off Beijing Regulatory Scrutiny
MU.description: NIO shares rallied 7.60% to $5.66 on Wednesday as risk appetite improved despite recent Beijing regulatory scrutiny on cross-border securities trading. The company posted strong Q1 results with 98.3% YoY delivery growth to 83,465 units and gross margin expansion to 19%, though sequential metrics showed weakness with a 33.1% drop in deliveries and 26.3% revenue decline. Technically, the stock remains in a tug-of-war zone between its moving averages with mixed momentum signals.

MU.headline: Micron Vs. SK Hynix: Which Of Wall Street's Trillion-Dollar Memory Stocks Win In 2026?
MU.description: Both Micron Technology and SK Hynix have crossed $1 trillion valuations as AI demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips surges. While SK Hynix currently dominates with 57% HBM market share and stronger Nvidia relationships, Micron is rapidly closing the gap with aggressive expansion plans, secured 2026 HBM supply agreements, and narrowing technology gaps. Both stocks trade at attractive valuations despite extraordinary gains, with Micron showing higher projected earnings growth for the next q

MU.headline: Record Profits, Not Hype: Goldman Says Nvidia And Micron Take The S&P 500 To 8,000
MU.description: Goldman Sachs raised its S&P 500 year-end target from 7,600 to 8,000, citing exceptionally strong Q1 earnings driven by AI infrastructure spending. The bank expects S&P 500 EPS to grow 24% in 2026 and 13% in 2027, with semiconductor stocks and hyperscalers accounting for roughly half of earnings growth. However, Goldman flagged risks including potential oil shocks and the need for AI spending to translate into sustainable productivity gains.

MU.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow Futures Rise Following Tech Rally—Zscaler, United Microelectronics, Braiin In Focus (UPDATED)
MU.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following a mixed Tuesday session where the Nasdaq advanced led by tech stocks while the Dow ended lower. Key movers include United Microelectronics jumping 11.24% after acquiring manufacturing equipment, Braiin tumbling 13.45% after a 74% surge, Box declining 2.42% despite beating earnings expectations, and Zscaler falling after missing revenue guidance. The 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.46%, with markets pricing a 99.9% likelihood of unchanged Fed rate

MU.headline: After Micron, Nvidia-Supplier SK Hynix Joins $1 Trillion Club As AI Chip Frenzy Reaches New Heights— Analyst Says Memory Boom Is Far From Over
MU.description: SK Hynix has become the third memory chip company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization, following Micron and Samsung. The surge is driven by soaring demand for high-end memory chips used in AI systems, with memory chip prices doubling in Q1 and expected to rise 63% this quarter. Analysts predict memory chip demand will exceed supply through 2028, keeping prices elevated.

MU.headline: Has Wall Street Found AI’s New Choke Point?
MU.description: Memory chip manufacturers SK Hynix and Micron have reached $1 trillion valuations, signaling a major shift in how Wall Street values AI infrastructure. While Nvidia remains important, investors increasingly recognize that control of memory chips and hardware bottlenecks—rather than AI model builders—may be where the real pricing power lies. High-bandwidth memory is essential for AI systems at scale, making semiconductor suppliers critical to the AI boom.

OUST.headline: Ouster Stock Hits 52-Week High - Here's Why
OUST.description: Ouster (NASDAQ:OUST) stock surged to a 52-week high, up 16.77% to $43.24, following a strategic partnership announcement with ARGUS Interception GmbH to integrate its digital lidar technology into drone defense systems. The rally was further supported by the company's Rev8 operating system achieving sensor qualification for NVIDIA's DRIVE Hyperion platform. Technical indicators show bullish momentum with the MACD above its signal line, though analysts maintain a Buy rating with a $41.50 price ta

BB.headline: US Stocks Show AI Breadth Is Replacing Defensive Leadership
BB.description: US equities rallied post-Memorial Day with strong AI and small-cap leadership, as de-escalation hopes in the US-Iran conflict drove oil prices lower. Micron Technology surged 18% to cross $1 trillion market cap on a UBS price target hike, while quantum computing and space stocks rallied on government funding and SpaceX IPO anticipation. The Nasdaq rose 1.11% and Russell 2000 jumped 1.74%, though the Dow declined 0.19% as defensive sectors unwound.

FUTU.headline: China's Hefty Fines Remove Key Overhang For Futu And UP Fintech
FUTU.description: China's securities regulator fined Futu Holdings $271 million and UP Fintech $60 million for operating unlicensed cross-border trading services. While the penalties hurt short-term, they remove years of regulatory uncertainty, allowing both companies to focus on international expansion where they've already built strong presences in Hong Kong, Singapore, and other markets. Both companies have successfully diversified away from China, which now represents only 10-13% of their business.

FUTU.headline: China Cracks Down On Overseas Investing After Record $1 Trillion Capital Flight To US, Hong Kong Markets
FUTU.description: China has imposed restrictions on cross-border trading to combat record capital outflows of approximately $1 trillion in 2025, with Chinese investors moving funds into U.S. and Hong Kong markets. The China Securities Regulatory Commission has penalized offshore brokers Tiger Brokers, Futu, and Longbridge Securities with combined fines of $330 million for illegal cross-border operations. The crackdown could affect up to HK$250 billion in Hong Kong assets.

DELL.headline: IREN Bets Big on Dell as It Races to Rewrite Its Own Story
DELL.description: IREN, a former Bitcoin miner, is rapidly transforming into an AI cloud infrastructure company. The company signed a $1.6 billion procurement agreement with Dell for GPUs and equipment to support its growing AI cloud operations. IREN has secured major contracts including a $9.7 billion five-year deal with Microsoft and a $3.4 billion contract with Nvidia, positioning it with $3.1 billion in contracted annualized recurring revenue. However, recent quarterly results showed a 34% revenue miss and si

DELL.headline: US Stocks Show AI Breadth Is Replacing Defensive Leadership
DELL.description: US equities rallied post-Memorial Day with strong AI and small-cap leadership, as de-escalation hopes in the US-Iran conflict drove oil prices lower. Micron Technology surged 18% to cross $1 trillion market cap on a UBS price target hike, while quantum computing and space stocks rallied on government funding and SpaceX IPO anticipation. The Nasdaq rose 1.11% and Russell 2000 jumped 1.74%, though the Dow declined 0.19% as defensive sectors unwound.

DELL.headline: Cloud-Flation: Dell’s On-Premise AI Bet Pays Off
DELL.description: A fundamental shift in AI infrastructure is underway as enterprises move from centralized cloud data centers to on-premise hardware due to unsustainable token consumption costs (320x increase). Dell Technologies is positioned to dominate this transition through its AI Factory partnership with NVIDIA, having secured over 5,000 enterprise customers with 1,000 acquired in Q1 FY2027. The company's $43 billion backlog and 95% year-to-date stock surge reflect strong momentum, though valuation concerns

DELL.headline: These Are the AI Trades I'd Make Today if I Were Starting My Portfolio From Scratch
DELL.description: An investment analyst recommends three AI stocks for a fresh portfolio: Qualcomm for its edge AI and data center chip opportunities, Dell for its AI-optimized server infrastructure business, and ServiceNow for its workflow automation software with strong growth and profitability despite recent stock weakness.

DELL.headline: Five Things Moving Markets: Records, Record Lows, And $100 Oil All In One Week
DELL.description: Markets hit record highs with the S&P 500 posting its eighth straight weekly gain and the Dow closing at 50,579.70, while consumer sentiment fell to record lows. Oil prices remain elevated above $103 amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Fed officials signal no near-term rate cuts, with Waller calling rate-cut talk 'crazy.' Dell and HP surged on AI server demand, while SpaceX filed for IPO with major institutional interest.

QBTS.headline: The U.S. Government Just Invested in These 3 Quantum Computing Stocks. Should Retail Investors Follow Suit?
QBTS.description: The U.S. government announced a $2 billion investment in the quantum computing sector, with up to $100 million each going to D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion. While the stocks surged on the news, an analyst cautions that all three remain highly speculative investments with significant technical challenges and unproven commercialization paths.

QBTS.headline: Quantum Computing’s Commercial Breakout Has Arrived
QBTS.description: The quantum computing sector is transitioning from theoretical research to commercial viability, driven by enterprise adoption and recurring cloud-based revenue models. Infrastructure players like IBM and GlobalFoundries are building manufacturing capacity, while pure-play operators like D-Wave and Rigetti are shifting to quantum-as-a-service models with strong booking growth, though they face near-term cash burn challenges.

QBTS.headline: Why D-Wave Quantum Stock Plummeted Today
QBTS.description: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) stock fell 5.3% on May 26, 2026, despite broader market gains, as investors took profits after a 41.5% three-month rally and reacted to concerns about the company's simulation data leadership claims. While D-Wave issued a response defending its competitive position, the stock remains under pressure due to competitive risks and high valuation concerns at 242.8x expected sales.

QBTS.headline: The U.S. Government Just Bet $2 Billion on 9 Quantum Computing Companies. Here's What It Means for Investors.
QBTS.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce announced $2.01 billion in CHIPS Act funding for nine quantum computing companies, with IBM receiving $1 billion for a new quantum chip foundry. While IBM stock jumped 12%, smaller quantum companies like D-Wave, Rigetti, and Infleqtion surged over 30% despite minimal revenues and significant losses. The article cautions that these are letters of intent, not finalized deals, and warns investors that smaller quantum companies remain highly speculative with valuation

FLY.headline: Firefly Aerospace Stock Soars On SpaceX IPO And Starship V3 Launch
FLY.description: Firefly Aerospace (FLY) shares surged 24.06% following SpaceX's successful Starship V3 test flight and the company's IPO filing seeking up to $75 billion at a potential $2 trillion valuation. The test demonstrated all 33 Raptor engines firing, successful space deployment, and heat shield separation. SpaceX's IPO prospectus, expected to list on Nasdaq around June 12 under ticker SPCX, has boosted broader space sector sentiment.

ARM.headline: Nvidia has a $200 Billion Warning for AMD and Intel Stock Investors
ARM.description: Nvidia is entering the standalone server CPU market with its next-generation Vera processor, projecting $20 billion in revenue by 2027 from a $200 billion total addressable market. Using Arm-based architecture, Nvidia could capture significant market share from AMD and Intel, who currently dominate the x86-based server CPU space. This expansion threatens AMD and Intel's lucrative data center CPU business as hyperscalers increasingly prefer Arm architecture for cost efficiency.
